industries.name: Fertilizers & Agriculture
uses: Phosphate rock (fluorapatite, Ca₅(PO₄)₃F) mined and acidulated with sulfuric acid to produce phosphoric acid (H₃PO₄) — the key intermediate for all phosphate fertilizers
Diammonium phosphate (DAP, (NH₄)₂HPO₄) — the world's most widely traded phosphate fertilizer, supplying both phosphorus and nitrogen; applied to corn, wheat, soybeans, and rice
Monoammonium phosphate (MAP, NH₄H₂PO₄) — solid granular fertilizer used in blended NPK formulations for starter fertilizers and broadcast application
Triple superphosphate (TSP, Ca(H₂PO₄)₂) produced by reacting phosphate rock with phosphoric acid — higher phosphorus concentration than ordinary superphosphate
Phosphorus is one of the three macronutrients essential for all plant life (NPK) — drives root development, energy transfer (ATP), and seed formation; no agricultural substitute exists

companies.name: Mosaic Company
companies.use: World's largest combined phosphate and potash producer. Mines phosphate rock in Florida (Four Corners, South Fort Meade) and Brazil (Miski Mayo). Produces DAP, MAP, and MicroEssentials specialty fertilizers.

companies.name: OCP Group
companies.use: World's largest phosphate exporter. Morocco holds ~70% of world phosphate reserves. OCP mines at Khouribga, Gantour, and Bou Craa and refines to phosphoric acid and fertilizers at Jorf Lasfar.

companies.name: PhosAgro
companies.use: Russia's largest phosphate company. Mines apatite-nepheline ore at Kirovsk (Kola Peninsula) and produces DAP, MAP, and NPK fertilizers. Major supplier to European and Asian agricultural markets.

companies.name: Nutrien
companies.use: World's largest crop nutrient company. Operates phosphate mines in North Carolina and Idaho and produces DAP and MAP fertilizers. Distributes through the world's largest retail agricultural network.

companies.name: Yara International
companies.use: World's largest nitrogen fertilizer company and major blender of NPK fertilizers containing phosphate. Distributes phosphate fertilizers globally through its extensive logistics and retail network.

industries.name: Food & Beverage Processing
uses: Phosphoric acid (food-grade H₃PO₄, E338) as acidulant in cola beverages — gives Coca-Cola and Pepsi their characteristic sharp tartness; US cola industry uses ~100,000 tonnes/yr
Sodium and calcium phosphates as emulsifying salts in processed cheese (American cheese, cheese slices) — bind proteins and prevent oil separation during melting
Sodium tripolyphosphate (STPP, Na₅P₃O₁₀) as moisture-retention agent in processed shrimp, scallops, and poultry — absorbs water to increase product weight
Monocalcium phosphate (MCP) as leavening acid in baking powder and self-rising flour — reacts with sodium bicarbonate to release CO₂
Dicalcium phosphate as calcium supplement and anti-caking agent in breakfast cereals, infant formula, and dietary supplements

companies.name: ICL Group (ICL Food Specialties)
companies.use: World's largest food-grade phosphate producer. Supplies phosphoric acid to Coca-Cola and PepsiCo, and emulsifying salts to dairy and meat processors globally under the JOHA and BK Giulini brands.

companies.name: Innophos Holdings
companies.share: ~15% of North American food phosphate market
companies.use: Major US producer of specialty phosphates for food, beverage, and nutrition applications. Produces phosphoric acid, leavening agents, emulsifiers, and mineral supplements at plants in US and Mexico.

companies.name: Prayon
companies.use: European phosphoric acid and specialty phosphate producer. Supplies food-grade phosphates for beverages, dairy, meat processing, and bakery to European food manufacturers.

industries.name: Industrial & Technical Phosphates
uses: Organophosphate flame retardants (e.g. TCPP, TDCPP, resorcinol bis-diphenyl phosphate) for flexible and rigid polyurethane foam in furniture, mattresses, and building insulation
Phosphate ester hydraulic fluids for fire-resistant applications in steel mills, aircraft hydraulics, and underground mining equipment
Phosphoric acid in metal surface treatment (phosphating) — creates zinc or manganese phosphate conversion coatings on steel for corrosion protection and paint adhesion
Trisodium phosphate (TSP) as heavy-duty cleaner and degreaser for industrial equipment, dairy plant sanitation, and surface preparation before painting
Phosphorus pentasulfide (P₄S₁₀) as precursor for lubricant additives (zinc dialkyldithiophosphate, ZDDP) — anti-wear additive in virtually all motor oils

companies.name: Lanxess
companies.use: Major producer of organophosphate flame retardants and phosphate ester fluids for polymer and industrial fluid applications under the Levagard and Disflamoll brands.

companies.name: Clariant
companies.use: Specialty chemicals company producing phosphorus-based flame retardants for textiles, electronics, and polymer applications as part of its functional minerals and additives portfolio.

companies.name: Chemetall (BASF)
companies.use: Leading supplier of metal phosphating systems for automotive, aerospace, and industrial coating pretreatment. Processes steel, aluminum, and zinc surfaces to create phosphate conversion coatings for paint adhesion.

industries.name: Detergents & Water Treatment
industries.share: ~5% of phosphorus use (declining — phosphates banned in laundry detergents in most developed countries)
uses: Sodium tripolyphosphate (STPP) was the dominant laundry and dishwasher detergent builder — softens water by sequestering Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ ions that deactivate surfactants; banned in laundry detergents across EU and US due to eutrophication
Phosphate-based detergents remain permitted and widely used for automatic dishwashing in most countries where phosphate removal at wastewater treatment plants is not universal
Phosphonates (HEDP, ATMP) as scale inhibitors and corrosion inhibitors in cooling water systems, boilers, and reverse osmosis membranes
Sodium hexametaphosphate as sequestrant and dispersant in industrial cleaning, textile processing, and paper manufacturing

companies.name: Procter & Gamble
companies.use: Major consumer of food-grade and technical phosphates in dishwasher detergents (Cascade, Fairy Platinum) where phosphate use remains permitted. Has reformulated laundry products globally to remove phosphates.

companies.name: Thermphos International (now ICL)
companies.use: Major European phosphoric acid and technical phosphate producer supplying STPP for industrial detergents and HEDP phosphonate scale inhibitors for water treatment applications.

companies.name: Veolia Water Technologies
companies.use: Major operator of wastewater treatment plants that must remove phosphorus (both detergent and agricultural runoff) via biological nutrient removal and chemical precipitation with iron or aluminum salts to prevent eutrophication of waterways.
